blurb: Built from her real papers. Her Exam I is 31 best-answer multiple-choice questions at 3 points each, taken on Canvas in the lecture room, plus a separate 10-point collaborative exam in discussion. The codon and wobble tables are provided — they are below, in her orientation. Sit it timed, then review every question: each wrong option gets its own explanation.

papers.title: Exam I · Practice Paper A
papers.blurb: 34 multiple choice at 3 points each plus 2 written multi-step problems. Slightly longer than the real paper on purpose. Weighted to what she actually repeats year on year: wobble and illegal anticodons (her biggest bucket), replication mechanics, the guaranteed interrupted-mating map, repair-pathway routing, blots and EMSA, Sanger gels, primer design, and the plus/minus notation trap.
